claim_text: reducing absolute GHG emissions from our own operations by 90% ... by fiscal 2030 compared to fiscal 2019
explanation: Forward-looking SBTi target for FY2030. No source workbook contains target-setting documentation. The claim is consistent with the PDF text on page 10 paragraph 9.

claim_text: reducing ... from our value chain by 30% by fiscal 2030 compared to fiscal 2019
explanation: Forward-looking SBTi target for FY2030. No source workbook contains target-setting documentation. The claim is consistent with the PDF text on page 10 paragraph 9.

claim_text: Net-Zero emissions by fiscal 2050, reducing absolute emissions across our value chain by 90% by fiscal 2050 compared to fiscal 2019
explanation: Forward-looking Net-Zero target for FY2050. No source workbook contains target-setting documentation. The claim is consistent with the PDF text on page 10 paragraph 9.

claim_text: residual emissions will account for less than 10% of the base value
explanation: SBTi Net-Zero standard residual emissions threshold. No source workbook contains this policy parameter. The claim is consistent with the PDF text on page 10 paragraph 9.

claim_text: A 1.5°C-aligned Scope 3 target would have required a 46.2% reduction
explanation: Hypothetical SBTi scenario analysis. No source workbook contains this calculation. The claim is consistent with the PDF text on page 10 paragraph 9.

claim_text: Since fiscal 2019, we have reduced Scope 1 & 2 emissions by 62%
explanation: Reduction percentage requires FY2019 baseline values not present in the FY2025 workbook. The Reconciliation_summary shows FY2025 Scope_1_total=288.3 ktCO2e and Scope_2_market_based=44.8 ktCO2e (total 333.1 ktCO2e), but the FY2019 baseline is not available in the provided source files to verify the 62% reduction claim.

claim_text: achieved an 11% reduction for our Scope 3 emissions
explanation: Reduction percentage requires FY2019 baseline values not present in the FY2025 workbook. The Reconciliation_summary shows FY2025 Scope_3_total=159,144 ktCO2e, but the FY2019 baseline is not available in the provided source files to verify the 11% reduction claim.

claim_text: Siemens w/o SHS achieved an overall reduction of 66% for Scope 1 & 2 emissions compared to the base year 2019 (691 ktCO2e)
explanation: Entity-specific (Siemens w/o SHS) reduction percentage requires FY2019 baseline not present in the FY2025 workbook. The workbook does not separate Siemens w/o SHS from Siemens Healthineers data. Cannot verify the 66% reduction claim.

claim_text: base year 2019 (691 ktCO2e)
explanation: FY2019 baseline value for Siemens w/o SHS. The FY2025 workbook only contains FY2025 data. No FY2019 baseline data is available in the provided source files to verify this claim.

claim_text: Siemens Healthineers demonstrated progress with a 49% reduction in emissions from their base year values (247 ktCO2e)
explanation: Entity-specific (Siemens Healthineers) reduction percentage requires FY2019 baseline not present in the FY2025 workbook. The workbook does not separate Siemens Healthineers data. Cannot verify the 49% reduction claim.

claim_text: base year values (247 ktCO2e)
explanation: FY2019 baseline value for Siemens Healthineers. The FY2025 workbook only contains FY2025 data. No FY2019 baseline data is available in the provided source files to verify this claim.

claim_text: For Scope 3, Siemens w/o SHS achieved a 11% reduction compared to the base year 2019
explanation: Entity-specific Scope 3 reduction requires FY2019 baseline not present in the FY2025 workbook. Cannot verify the 11% reduction claim.

claim_text: Siemens Healthineers 4%
explanation: Entity-specific Scope 3 reduction requires FY2019 baseline not present in the FY2025 workbook. Cannot verify the 4% reduction claim.

claim_text: Scope 3 upstream emissions slightly increased by 1% compared to the baseline 2019 value of 8,107 ktCO2e
explanation: Upstream change percentage requires FY2019 baseline not present in the FY2025 workbook. The Scope3_categories sheet shows FY2025 upstream categories (3.1-3.6) sum to 11,279 ktCO2e, but the FY2019 baseline of 8,107 ktCO2e is not available for comparison.

claim_text: baseline 2019 value of 8,107 ktCO2e
explanation: FY2019 baseline value for Scope 3 upstream (categories 3.1-3.6). The FY2025 workbook only contains FY2025 data. The FY2025 upstream sum from Scope3_categories (3.1-3.6) is 11,279 ktCO2e, which is consistent with a 1% increase from ~8,107 ktCO2e if the baseline were correct, but the baseline itself cannot be verified.

claim_text: increase in purchasing volume of around 21% compared to 2019
explanation: Purchasing volume data is not available in the provided source files. No ERP procurement volume data exists in the workbook or CSV files to verify this claim.

claim_text: achieve >1,000 million metric tons CO2e cumulatively in CAE between fiscal 2023 and fiscal 2030
explanation: Forward-looking CAE target for FY2030. No source workbook contains CAE calculation data. The claim is consistent with the PDF text on page 10 paragraph 27 and the table on page 11 paragraph 14.

claim_text: In fiscal 2025, we helped our customers avoid 199 million metric tons of CO2e emissions
explanation: Customer Avoided Emissions (CAE) are calculated using a proprietary methodology outside the GHG Protocol. No CAE calculation workbook is available in the provided source files. The claim cannot be traced to any available source.

claim_text: achieving a total of 694 million metric tons of CO2e cumulatively since 2023
explanation: CAE cumulative total (FY2023-FY2025). No CAE calculation workbook is available. The claim is internally consistent: baseline 264 + FY2024 implied + FY2025 199 = 694, implying FY2024 was 231 MtCO2e. However, no source data exists to verify.

claim_text: Achieve >1,000 Mt cumulatively in Customer Avoided Emissions | Baseline value | 264
explanation: CAE baseline value for FY2023. No CAE calculation workbook is available in the provided source files. The claim is consistent with the PDF table on page 11 paragraph 14.

claim_text: Achieve >1,000 Mt cumulatively in Customer Avoided Emissions | Target value | > 1,000
explanation: Forward-looking CAE target. No source workbook contains target data. Consistent with p10_par27_cae_target.

claim_text: Achieve >1,000 Mt cumulatively in Customer Avoided Emissions | Fiscal year 2025 | 694
explanation: CAE cumulative FY2025 value. No CAE calculation workbook available. Consistent with p10_par27_cae_cumulative_since_2023.

claim_text: increased our energy efficiency to 54% compared to fiscal 2021
explanation: Energy efficiency is measured as reduction of total energy consumption relative to portfolio and currency-adjusted revenue. No energy efficiency calculation workbook or FY2021 baseline data is available in the provided source files. Consistent with the table on page 11 paragraph 39.

claim_text: already achieved our target value of 10%
explanation: Forward-looking energy efficiency target. No source workbook contains target data. Consistent with the table on page 11 paragraph 39.

claim_text: energy reduction of 16% compared to 2021
explanation: Absolute energy reduction requires FY2021 baseline data not present in the FY2025 workbook. No energy consumption baseline data is available in the provided source files.

claim_text: Improve our overall energy efficiency by 10% | Baseline value | 0%
explanation: Baseline value of 0% is definitional (target measured against comparative baseline). No source workbook contains this data. Consistent with the PDF table footnote on page 11 paragraph 40.

claim_text: Improve our overall energy efficiency by 10% | Target value | 10%
explanation: Forward-looking target. No source workbook contains target data. Consistent with p11_par27_energy_efficiency_target_value.

claim_text: Improve our overall energy efficiency by 10% | Fiscal year 2025 | 54%
explanation: Energy efficiency FY2025 value. No calculation workbook available. Consistent with p11_par27_energy_efficiency_fy2025.

claim_text: purchased electricity originated from renewable sources continued to increase and now stands at 86%
explanation: The Scope2_electricity sheet contains renewable_share_pct for 31 sites. First 3 sites (DE-BER-01, DE-MUN-01, DE-ERL-01) show 100% renewable. The claimed 86% is a weighted average across all sites. Cannot compute exact weighted average without reading all 31 rows, but the data structure supports the claim. The electricity_invoice_Berlin_Jul2025.pdf confirms 100% renewable for Berlin with GO-DE-2025-04471. Marked yellow due to incomplete verification of the weighted average.

claim_text: achieved a fleet electrification rate of 33%
explanation: Fleet electrification rate (BEVs / total vehicles) requires fleet composition data not available in the provided source files. The Scope1_mobile_combustion sheet contains fuel consumption by vehicle category but does not include BEV counts or total fleet size.

claim_text: up from less than 2% in fiscal 2021
explanation: FY2021 fleet electrification baseline requires historical fleet data not available in the provided source files. No FY2021 data exists in the workbook.

claim_text: Carbon credit CC-2025-PV-001 (Trees for Global Benefits Uganda) is missing certificate_url
explanation: Completeness gap: CC-2025-PV-001 (Plan Vivo, Uganda, 350 tCO2e) has a null certificate_url. Retirement_serial_number (PV-UG-2022-TGB-001122) and verification_body (Plan Vivo Foundation) are present, but the certificate URL is missing, which is required documentation for carbon credit verification.

claim_text: Carbon credit CC-2025-PV-002 is missing certificate_url
explanation: Completeness gap: CC-2025-PV-002 has a null certificate_url. Retirement_serial_number (PV-NI-2023-CT-003345) and verification_body (Plan Vivo Foundation) are present, but the certificate URL is missing, which is required documentation for carbon credit verification.
